description: Visit ten of the Eastern Suburbs' most popular parks and parklands. Drive through or along the main park entrance — no need to leave the road for a bushwalk.

unlock_rules.notes: Parks are visited by car via main gates or perimeter roads (e.g. Grand Drive). Practise giving way to pedestrians and cyclists.

parks.name: Centennial Park
parks.description: Sydney's largest urban parkland — enter via Grand Drive near Federation Pavilion.
parks.supervisor_notes: Low speed, cyclists everywhere. Practise shared-space awareness on Grand Drive.
parks.entrance_hint: Federation Pavilion / Grand Drive main loop

parks.name: Queens Park
parks.description: Eastern edge of Centennial Parklands — open fields and sports ovals.
parks.supervisor_notes: Pair with Centennial Park in one lesson; enter from Darley Road or York Road.

parks.name: Moore Park
parks.description: Northern Parklands section — Kippax Lake and Anzac Parade frontage.
parks.supervisor_notes: Busier near SCG/Allianz on event days; pick a quiet weekday first.

parks.name: Hyde Park
parks.description: CBD park perimeter — Elizabeth Street or Park Street pass-by in the free zone.
parks.supervisor_notes: Drive the park edge; do not stop in bus lanes. Good city-confidence practice.

parks.name: Robertson Park
parks.description: Harbour-view park at Watsons Bay — pairs with the Coast Expedition northern endpoint.
parks.supervisor_notes: Very busy weekends; practise calm kerbside approach near the wharf.

parks.name: Nielsen Park
parks.description: Harbour beach park on Greycliffe Avenue — popular family picnic spot.
parks.supervisor_notes: Narrow approach road; watch for pedestrians crossing to the beach.

parks.name: Rushcutters Bay Park
parks.description: Harbour-side park on New South Head Road — yachts, joggers, and café traffic.
parks.supervisor_notes: Practise merging back onto New South Head Road after the park pass.

parks.name: Bronte Park
parks.description: Classic beachside park with picnic shelters overlooking Bronte Beach.
parks.supervisor_notes: Summer weekends are packed; aim for a weekday or early morning stamp.

parks.name: Coogee Reserve
parks.description: Coastal reserve above Coogee Beach — includes Dunningham Reserve clifftop roads.
parks.supervisor_notes: Combine with Coogee car park or Arden Street hill badges in one coastal lesson.

parks.name: Kamay Botany Bay National Park (La Perouse)
parks.description: Southern park entry near La Perouse Point — caps the coastal park collection.
parks.supervisor_notes: Pairs with Coast Expedition southern endpoint. Watch for tourists near Bare Island.

honourable_mentions.name: Waverley Park
honourable_mentions.note: Famous playground near Bondi — good extension for a Bondi-focused pack.

honourable_mentions.name: Marks Park
honourable_mentions.note: Coastal walk stop with clifftop views — drive-by on Military Road.

honourable_mentions.name: Heffron Park
honourable_mentions.note: Large sports fields — wide roads, good for low-pressure practice.

_meta.coordinate_note: Bounding boxes are approximate for v1. Verify at main park entrances.
_meta.visit_note: Drive through the main park gate or perimeter road; walking the interior is optional and not required.
